rulings.Case Title: LINDA COOKS VS. WELLS FARGO HOME MORTGAGE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to causes of action for negligence and UCL violation is OVERRULED. All elements of these causes of action are sufficiently alleged. Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to (1) causes of action for breach of contract and breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing for Plaintiff to allege a contract starting with formation (offer, acceptance, consideration), terms, whether it is written or oral, etc. If contract is written it shall be attached and the breach provision identified; if alleged contract is oral to address statute of frauds; (2) promissory estoppel for plaintiff to allege a clear promise and facts to support Plaintiff's change in position in reliance on that promise; (3) cause of action for negligent misrepresentation for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of this cause of action with requisite specificity; (4) cause of action for false reporting for Plaintiff to allege a reporting that was false, specifically to allege that Plaintiff was not in default/current on her loan at the time such a report was made. Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to (1) cause of action for accounting - Plaintiff failed to allege that "some balance is due the plaintiff" Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156 or cite any authority for the proposition that accounting cause of action may be asserted by a borrower against a lender. Plaintiff failed to assert any facts that may be alleged if leave to amend is granted; (2) cause of action for violation of CC 2923.6. Plaintiff failed to address Defendant's arguments regarding CC 2923.6(g) or CC 2923.6 being repealed. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 10 HAWTHORNE STREET, LLC VS. JOHN BERGGRUEN GALLERY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2018 line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN BERGGRUEN GALLERY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden. All of the issues submitted for adjudication depend on interpretation of Section 3.2 of the lease. Moving party failed to show that the calculation of the rental commencement date based in the formula provided in this section is applicable if possession is not delivered "for whatever reason" (Reply 3:3). Section 3.2 contains conditional language in the same sentence as the formula and the moving party fails to provide any argument or authority as to why that language should not be considered or given any consideration. Additionally there are triable issues of fact regarding possession. See facts 11 and 14. While moving party cites cases for the proposition that breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment may subject the landlord to liability for damages (e.g. Petroleum Collection v. Swords (1975) 48 Cal.App.3d 841; Guntert v. City of Stockton (1976) 55 Cal.App.3d 131); none of the authorities cited stand for the proposition that breach of this covenant indicates failure to deliver possession. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARIBELLE NAVARRO VS. VIKTOR NIKONETS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT VIKTOR NIKONETS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend based on uncertainty. Plaintiff shall either allege that the UD is based solely on expiration of the fixed term lease or allege all elements of UD based on the attached 30 Day Notice, including service and expiration of the notice (Proof of Service attached to the complaint is insufficient). = (501/CFH)
